children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a dam safety maintenance and repair program within the Bureau to ensure maintenance and monitoring of the condition of each dam identified pursuant to subsection (e) necessary to maintain the dam in a satisfactory condition on a long-term basis.

children.heading: Transfer of existing functions and personnel
children.label: b Transfer of existing functions and personnel
children.text: All functions performed before , pursuant to the Dam Safety Program established by the Secretary of the Interior by order dated , and all Bureau of Indian Affairs personnel assigned to such program as of , are hereby transferred to the Dam Safety Maintenance and Repair Program. Any reference in any law, regulation, executive order, reorganization plan, or delegation of authority to the Dam Safety Program is deemed to be a reference to the Dam Safety Maintenance and Repair Program.August 23, 1994February 28, 1980August 23, 1994

children.heading: Rehabilitation
children.label: c Rehabilitation
children.text: Under the Dam Safety Maintenance and Repair Program, the Secretary shall perform such rehabilitation work as is necessary to bring the dams identified pursuant to subsection (e) to a satisfactory condition. In addition, each dam located on Indian lands shall be regularly maintained pursuant to the Dam Safety Maintenance and Repair Program established pursuant to subsection (a).

children.heading: Maintenance action plan
children.label: d Maintenance action plan
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a maintenance action plan, which shall include a prioritization of actions to be taken, for those dams with a risk hazard rating of high or significant as identified pursuant to subsection (e).

children.heading: Development of list
children.label: 1 Development of list
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a comprehensive list of dams located on Indian lands that describes the dam safety condition classification of each dam, as specified in paragraph (2), the risk hazard classification of each dam, as specified in paragraph (3), and the conditions resulting from maintenance deficiencies.

children.heading: Satisfactory
children.label: A Satisfactory
children.text: No existing or potential dam safety deficiencies are recognized. Safe performance is expected under all anticipated conditions.

children.heading: Fair
children.label: B Fair
children.text: No existing dam safety deficiencies are recognized for normal loading conditions. Infrequent hydrologic or seismic events would probably result in a dam safety deficiency.

children.heading: Conditionally poor
children.label: C Conditionally poor
children.text: A potential dam safety deficiency is recognized for unusual loading conditions that may realistically occur during the expected life of the structure.

children.heading: Poor
children.label: D Poor
children.text: A potential dam safety deficiency is clearly recognized for normal loading conditions. Immediate actions to resolve the deficiency are recommended; reservoir restrictions may be necessary until resolution of the problem.

children.heading: Unsatisfactory
children.label: E Unsatisfactory
children.text: A dam safety deficiency exists for normal loading conditions. Immediate remedial action is required for resolution of the problem.

children.heading: High
children.label: A High
children.text: Six or more lives would be at risk or extensive property damage could occur if the dam failed.

children.heading: Significant
children.label: B Significant
children.text: Between one and six lives would be at risk or significant property damage could occur if the dam failed.

children.heading: Low
children.label: C Low
children.text: No lives would be at risk and limited property damage would occur if the dam failed.

children.heading: Limitation on program authorization
children.label: f Limitation on program authorization
children.text: Work authorized by this chapter shall be for the purpose of dam safety maintenance and structural repair. The Secretary may authorize, upon request of an Indian tribe, up to 20 percent of the cost of repairs to be used to provide additional conservation storage capacity or developing benefits beyond those provided by the original dams and reservoirs. This chapter is not intended to preclude development of increased storage or benefits under any other authority or to preclude measures to protect fish and wildlife.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: g Technical assistance
children.text: To carry out the purposes of this chapter, the Secretary may obtain technical assistance on a nonreimbursable basis from other departments and agencies. Notwithstanding any such technical assistance, the Dam Safety Maintenance and Repair Program established under subsection (a) shall be under the direction and control of the Bureau.

children.heading: Contract authority
children.label: h Contract authority
children.text: In addition to any other authority established by law, the Secretary is authorized to contract with Indian tribes under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.) to carry out the Dam Safety Maintenance and Repair Program established under this chapter.25 U.S.C. 4501See References in Text note below.1
the list of dams and their status on the maintenance action plan developed under this section; and
the projected total cost and a schedule of the projected annual cost of rehabilitation or repair for each dam under this section.
